metric: # MPS feasibility on this host: UNAVAILABLE. nvidia-cuda-mps-control / nvidia-cuda-mps-server do not exist anywhere on the filesystem (full-fs find empty; dpkg -S no owning pkg). Host is WSL2 (6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2); GPU is paravirtualized through the Windows host driver via /usr/lib/wsl/lib (libcuda.so.1, libnvidia-ml.so.1, nvidia-smi only). The MPS daemon ships with the Linux NVIDIA driver package, which WSL2 does not install. `nvidia-cuda-mps-control -d` => "command not found" (exit 127). GPU Compute Mode = Default throughout. No mps-on rows can be measured on this host; that is the answer (deployment lever absent — needs bare-metal Linux).

# The mps_state=off rows below are the measured #140 baseline (benchmarks/gpu_coldstart_2026-05-29.tsv, 7 fresh processes per cell, warm PTX disk cache, RTX 5070, driver 596.21, cubecl 0.10.1, git=0caf36d5). They are the real per-process context-init floor MPS would have been compared against. No values are fabricated; mps-on cells are intentionally absent.
